summary: Drew DeVault shares his July 2020 progress update, noting reduced output due to taking a week off. The highlights include the creation of the BARE message format, which quickly gained five community implementations in different languages. He also mentions new non-blog content on his site, including pages on static linking misconceptions and text editing videos. The main development news is the addition of continuous integration to SourceHut's mailing lists, enabled by the project hub.
key_points: The BARE message format was created and quickly adopted with five independent implementations in Rust, Python, JavaScript, D, and Zig
SourceHut mailing lists now support continuous integration, a milestone enabled by the project hub
New static content was added to drewdevault.com outside the blog/RSS system, including pages on static linking and text editing
money_quotes: One was the invention of the BARE message format, which I wrote a blog post about if you want to learn more.
Since that article, five new implementations have appeared from various authors: Rust, Python, JavaScript, D, and Zig.
Perhaps the most exciting development is the addition of continuous integration to the mailing lists.
It's the first of many features which are now possible thanks to the addition of the project hub.
Some time off was much-needed, though.
key_insight: The BARE message format's rapid community adoption across five languages and CI integration for SourceHut mailing lists represent meaningful infrastructure progress in the open-source ecosystem.
